body:
i have the same issue. APK builds successfully with all those warnings/errors. Possibly linked to this [https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/170534](url)

a temp solution is to add this to your `gradle.properties` files. The default value is `true`, so making this to `false` will make build-time longer.

`
kotlin.incremental=false
`

After more testing, these gradle/kotlin version seem to work and not show the error. 


```
// settings.gradle

plugins {
    id "dev.flutter.flutter-plugin-loader" version "1.0.0"
    id "com.android.application" version "8.3.2" apply false
    // START: FlutterFire Configuration
    id "com.google.gms.google-services" version "4.3.15" apply false
    // END: FlutterFire Configuration
    id "org.jetbrains.kotlin.android" version "1.9.0" apply false
}
```



```
// gradle-wrapper.properties

distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.12-all.zip

```

```
// gradle.properties

org.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx4g
#android.enableR8=true
android.useAndroidX=true
android.enableJetifier=true
kotlin.incremental=true
```

> Caused by: java.lang.AssertionError: java.lang.Exception: Could not close incremental caches in D:\Programming\Flutter\flutter_daemoncomp_test\build\device_info_plus\kotlin\compileReleaseKotlin\cacheable\caches-jvm\jvm\kotlin: class-fq-name-to-source.tab, source-to-classes.tab, internal-name-to-source.tab
> 	at org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.google.common.io.Closer.close(Closer.java:236)

@Dionysius1984 This is pointing to community package, so you'll probably need to raise this in dedicated repo or adjust your `build.gradle` that is compatible with this plugin's requirement. 
Closing for now. Also check if you still get same behavior without using `device_info_plus` or not. If not, then this could be related to the plugin and raise there as applicable. If you disagree, write in comments and I'll reopen it.

I do not agree. The issue happens as soon as I add 1 of the packages or both. To me this looks like an incompatability between the packages and Kotlin. I will also see where I can ask this same question to them in the mean time.
saya sempat mengalami 1 kesalahan ini ini membantu saya sampai jadi membuat website [informasimu.com](https://informasimu.com/)
Thanks for the feedback. Reopening for further analysis.

@Dionysius1984 
I tried to replicate with by creating new Flutter project and first only add `shared_preferences` with which I didn't get any issues as reported. Then I added `device_info_plus` which also didn't give me reported log, so I am unable to replicate it.


> The issue happens as soon as I add 1 of the packages or both.

Does the same error / log occurs if you only add `shared_preferences` ? If so, please provide us relevant error log.

I started again with a new flutter project. I made no changes and ran flutter build apk. On this I got no errors.
Then I added shared preferences and I got the error about my ndkversion. Then the error logging appeared. I added my error log. 
Do you need anything else? i also have debug_info, but I can't attach this. 

[errors-1754990560659.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21732901/errors-1754990560659.log)

I am running via VSCode. So I ran via cmd here is the output:
```
D:\Programming\Flutter\flutter_daemoncomp_test>flutter build apk
Font asset "MaterialIcons-Regular.otf" was tree-shaken, reducing it from 1645184 to 1368 bytes (99.9% reduction). Tree-shaking can be disabled by providing the --no-tree-shake-icons flag when building your app.
Running Gradle task 'assembleRelease'...                         1380.7s
√ Built build\app\outputs\flutter-apk\app-release.apk (18.9MB)
I see no errors. and my error log is also not updated in the android\.kotlin\errors folder
```

Using ndkVersion = flutter.ndkVersion in my build.gradle.kts gives the below error log. 

```
Your project is configured with Android NDK 26.3.11579264, but the following plugin(s) depend on a different Android NDK version:
- device_info_plus requires Android NDK 27.0.12077973
- shared_preferences_android requires Android NDK 27.0.12077973
Fix this issue by using the highest Android NDK version (they are backward compatible).
Add the following to D:\Programming\Flutter\flutter_daemoncomp_test\android\app\build.gradle.kts:

    android {
        ndkVersion = "27.0.12077973"
        ...
    }
```

Hope this helps. If you need more let me know.

> Your project is configured with Android NDK 26.3.11579264, but the following plugin(s) depend on a different Android NDK version:
> - device_info_plus requires Android NDK 27.0.12077973
> - shared_preferences_android requires Android NDK 27.0.12077973
> Fix this issue by using the highest Android NDK version (they are backward compatible).
> Add the following to D:\Programming\Flutter\flutter_daemoncomp_test\android\app\build.gradle.kts:
> 
>     android {
>         ndkVersion = "27.0.12077973"
>         ...
>     }

This is already fixed as part of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/engine/src/flutter/tools/android_sdk/packages.txt but this change may not be in latest stable yet.



> I see no errors. and my error log is also not update

This probably indicates VScode issue ? Is there a way for you to check if this also occurs with other IDE ex: Android studio ? I did try using it and was unable to replicate.

Also, if you are not using latest shared_preferences plugin version, please do so and then re-run to confirm if you still see same error or not.

Alright then i'll wait for this next update, but it's no issue to manually change the ndkversion.

But this would mean it's not just a vscode issue.

java.lang.Exception

This I am unable to replicate running with new Flutter project on stable and master using AS, but based on the report and above  error log, I'll keep the issue open for team's tracking.

I'm getting the same error after updating to Flutter 3.35.x.
Literally same error log. 
Project build complains about `shared_preferences`, `share_plus` and `device_info_plus`, but builds and works fine.
```
plugins {
    id "dev.flutter.flutter-plugin-loader" version "1.0.0"
    id "com.android.application" version "8.7.0" apply false
    id "org.jetbrains.kotlin.android" version "2.1.0" apply false
}
```
```
distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.12-all.zip
```

I had the same error in the console on my Windows 11 machine with latest windows-updates.
In my project I really only let flutter generate the counter-app and added the `shared_preferences`-dependency.
For unknown reasons compiling to an appbundle worked in the terminal in VS-code but not in any other terminal-window.
I did not try it on the `C:`-drive, I always used the drive `E:`.
I could reproduce exact the same behavior (the error, the fact that it is unexpectedly working in VS-code) on another machine (where I also did this on `E:`).

I use this version(s):

```
>flutter --version
Flutter 3.32.8 • channel stable • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision edada7c56e (7 weeks ago) • 2025-07-25 14:08:03 +0000
Engine • revision ef0cd00091 (7 weeks ago) • 2025-07-24 12:23:50 -0700
Tools • Dart 3.8.1 • DevTools 2.45.1
```

After a lot of trouble shooting I finally got it working.

Just for reference, I hope it can help someone else, here are the steps I did to fix it:

- In `android\app\build.gradle.kts` I added `android { ndkVersion = "27.0.12077973" }` (something like run `sdkmanager "ndk;27.0.12077973"` may be required)
- In `android\gradle.properties` I added `android.ndkVersion=27.0.12077973`
- In `pubspec.yaml` I set all versions explictly without `^`: `sdk: 3.8.1`, `shared_preferences: 2.3.3`, `cupertino_icons: 1.0.8`, `flutter_lints: 5.0.0`.
- On the drive where the project is located on, create an empty folder somewhere (for example `E:\Misc\Cache`). Then create an environment-variable called `PUB_CACHE` and set the created folder as value for the environment-variable. (Do not forget to restart all terminal-windows to load the new environment-variable.)
- Then `flutter clean`

After that all `flutter build appbundle` worked not only in VS-code but also in the usual terminal.

This happens to me as well
and not only with that plugins, but with many, if not all...
